items.title: Deadline Day: Letschert van China naar AFC, NAC heeft nieuwe spits
items.summary: Het is Deadline Day in Nederland, voor de VriendenLoterij Eredivisie en Keuken Kampioen Divisie. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers op de laatste transferdag van Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland , waar het gisteren Deadline Day was.

items.title: Why Liverpool's January transfer window makes no sense
items.summary: Liverpool's lack of depth in defence has been a known issue since last summer, with stars like Virgil van Dijk playing every game. So why didn't they add reinforcements in January?

items.title: Transfer window winners and losers: How Man City, Liverpool, PSG, others fared
items.summary: Deadline day was extremely quiet and the winter transfer window as a whole had its share of perplexing moves.

items.title: 'Grumpy' Guardiola jokes about lack of City spending
items.summary: Pep Guardiola mischievously said he is "a little bit sad and upset" that Manchester City have only the seventh highest net spend on transfers in the Premier League over the last five years.

items.title: Pep: Pressure on City's big-spending PL rivals
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has said the pressure is on six of Manchester City's Premier League rivals to win more trophies because they have had a higher net spend.

items.title: Fener blame Al Ittihad for Kante transfer fiasco
items.summary: N'Golo Kanté's switch from Al Ittihad to Fenerbahce has collapsed, the day after the transfer window closed.

items.title: Benzema joins Al Hilal from Al Ittihad
items.summary: Karim Benzema has left Al Ittihad and signed a contract with Saudi Pro League rivals Al Hilal, the club announced Monday.

items.title: Schreuder beschermt Sano na transferstorm: 'Hij blijft er heel rustig onder'
items.summary: Na hectische transferdagen gaat bij NEC het vizier op het bekertoernooi. FC Volendam komt woensdag op bezoek in de kwartfinale. Trainer Dick Schreuder blikt vooruit en bespreekt natuurlijk de situatie van Kodai Sano, waar de afgelopen dagen veelvuldig aan werd getrokken door clubs uit binnen- en buitenland. 'Hij blijft er heel rustig onder en gaat er goed mee om.'

items.title: Franse WK-winnaar lacht Liverpool uit na miljoenentransfer: 'Sukkels'
items.summary: Christophe Dugarry heeft met verbazing gekeken naar de transfer van Jérémy Jacquet naar Liverpool. De oud-aanvaller, die met Frankrijk het WK 1998 en EK 2000 won, begrijpt niets van het bedrag (60 miljoen euro) dat The Reds aan Stade Rennais overmaken.

items.title: Mateta faces uncertain future for club and country
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at what next for Jean-Philippe Mateta after his dream move to AC Milan broke down over issues with his medical

items.title: VI Live: AZ verdubbelt de voorsprong op Dortmund in Youth League
items.summary: Dinsdagavond staat de eerste wedstrijd in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker op het programma. Om 20.00 uur staan AZ en FC Twente tegenover elkaar. Welke ploeg zich als eerst halvefinalist mag noemen, volg je in deze VI Live.

items.title: Williams reveal reworked look for 2026 season
items.summary: Williams have revealed their livery for the 2026 Formula 1 season.

items.title: The talented playmaker no-one seems to want
items.summary: Harvey Elliott is one of England's most gifted young players - but neither Aston Villa or Liverpool appear to rate the midfielder.

items.title: HSV schorst buitenspeler en geeft hem recordboete
items.summary: HSV heeft Jean-Luc Dompé tot nader order geschorst. De dertigjarige buitenspeler werd aangehouden met alcohol op achter het stuur. Dompé heeft tevens een recordboete van de club gehad, bestaande uit 'een bedrag met zes cijfers'.

items.title: Newey: Aston Martin on 'back foot' by four months
items.summary: Aston Martin boss Adrian Newey has revealed his team have been on the "back foot" by four months.

items.title: Scorecard: Sri Lanka vs England, third T20
ITV heads to New York for World Cup coverage while BBC stays in Salford
items.summary: ITV Sport opts for studio in Brooklyn BBC will stay in UK until at least quarter-finals ITV has gained an early advantage over the BBC before their ratings battle at this summer’s World Cup by securing a studio in Brooklyn with views of the Manhattan skyline and Brooklyn Bridge. ITV Sport will be basing all of its World Cup coverage, to be presented by Mark Pougatch and Laura Woods, from its New York studio, whereas the BBC team of Mark Chapman, Gabby Logan and Kelly Cates will be in Salford until at least the quarter-finals. The BBC’s decision to stay in the United Kingdom was based on a combination of financial and environmental factors, with the corporation committed to limiting air travel in an attempt to reduce its carbon footprint. Continue reading...

items.title: England tumble to 60-6 in final T20 vs Sri Lanka LIVE!
Brown Girl Sport continues to cut through isolation and provide support
items.summary: Organisation formed by journalist Miriam Walker-Khan is taking the next step in making sure South Asian women and girls feel welcome in football at all levels There was a different kind of energy in an upstairs room at Stamford Bridge after escaping the buzz of the match-going crowd before Arsenal’s 2-0 defeat of Chelsea in the Women’s Super League 10 days ago. There was a celebratory, empowering energy, but also a determined and hopeful vibe. The room was full of people celebrating the third anniversary of Brown Girl Sport, the award-winning online platform and community that aims to highlight the stories of South Asian women and girls in sport in order, according to its website, “to smash stereotypes that Brown women don’t do, care or know about sport”. Continue reading...
